text: The X axis is time. What's the Y axis?

elements.text: The X axis is time. What's the Y axis?

text: Technological inertia. Which I think is roughly proportional to the number of non-tech users.

elements.text: Technological inertia. Which I think is roughly proportional to the number of non-tech users.

text: <@UGWUJUZHT> Yes, commoditization started earlier. The Unix workstations that I used in the 1990s were already commodities in a narrower sense. They were reserved to professional use because of their cost. But there was already off-the-shelf application software, which for many buyers was important enough to be a criterion for choosing one brand over another.

elements.text: Yes, commoditization started earlier. The Unix workstations that I used in the 1990s were already commodities in a narrower sense. They were reserved to professional use because of their cost. But there was already off-the-shelf application software, which for many buyers was important enough to be a criterion for choosing one brand over another.
